sanskrit_devanagari:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन ।
कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥ ७ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate 'rjuna |
karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate || 7 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: On the other hand, if a sincere person tries to control the active senses by the mind and begins karma-yoga [in Kṛṣṇa consciousness] without attachment, he is by far superior.
On the other hand, if a sincere person tries to control the active senses by the mind and begins karma-yoga [in Krishna consciousness] without attachment, he is by far superior.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 963 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Instead of becoming a pseudo transcendentalist for the sake of wanton living and sense enjoyment, it is far better to remain in one’s own business and execute the purpose of life, which is to get free from material bondage and enter into the kingdom of God.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.7 Tu, but, on the other hand, O Arjuna; yah, one who is unenlightened and who is eligible for action; arabhate, engages in;-what does he engage in? the Lord says in answer-karma yogam, Karma-yoga; karma-indriyaih, with the organs of action, with speech, hands, etc.; niyamya, controlling; indriyani…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-3-7-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: The Advaita reading: 3.7 is superior to the path of 3.6 (defective renunciation). The karma-yogin who has actually controlled the senses with the mind, then engages the action-organs in karma-yoga without attachment — this is the genuine path, free from the hypocrite's defects. The pairing 3.6/3.7 makes karma-yoga's superiority over false-renunciation explicit.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.7 Consequently, he who, with aspiration to have the vision of the self, directs his senses to action according to the scriptures, such action being of the same class as those which he practised earlier, and who then begins to practise Karma Yoga, after renouncing attachment, with the senses which…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-3-7-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: The Vishishtadvaita reading: 3.7 is the karma-yogin who has actually subdued the inner organ: senses controlled by the mind, action-organs engaged in karma-yoga, all without attachment. This person is viśiṣyate — distinguished, superior — over the hypocrite of 3.6. The path is not external withdrawal but real interior discipline issuing into engaged action.

positions.rendering: this karma-yoga, free from such defects, is superior
this karma-yogin is distinguished / superior to the hypocrite of 3.6

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All four agree viśiṣyate = is the distinguished one, is superior. The disagreement is about the SCOPE of the comparison. The Advaita reading takes it as karma-yoga vs. defective renunciation (the path of 3.6); the Vishishtadvaita reading takes it as the karma-yogin vs. the hypocrite specifically. Both readings preserve the structural pairing of 3.6 and 3.7 — false withdrawal contrasted with genuine attention-discipline that issues into action.

literal_meaning: But, O addressed-one, one who controls the senses with the mind and, unattached, undertakes karma-yoga with the action-organs is the superior one.
traditional_meaning_consensus: 3.7 inverts 3.6. yas tu — but the one who, having controlled the senses with the mind (manasā niyamya), engages the action-organs in karma-yoga (karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam) without attachment — that one is viśiṣyate (the distinguished, the superior). Per the Advaita reading the path here is not superficial sense-restraint but real attention-discipline that issues into engaged unattached action. Per the Vishishtadvaita reading the seeker who has subdued the inner instrument can perform karma-yoga genuinely; this superiority is over the hypocrite of 3.6. The verse pairs with 3.6: do not be the false renunciate; be the karma-yogin whose senses are actually controlled.
